preview_text:
The model rates Netherlands as clear favorite with a 68% win probability, leaving Sweden only an 8% chance of victory. This 59-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial disparity in the forecast. Sweden's underdog status is pronounced under the statistical framework.

The draw emerges as a meaningful outcome in both projections, hovering near 24%. The W/D/L spread shows Netherlands favored but not overwhelmingly so once draws are acknowledged; competitive matches at this level produce draws roughly one-quarter of the time. The model sees a moderate gap between the two sides rather than a mismatch.

Model and market diverge noticeably on Sweden's prospects. The model assigns Sweden 8% win probability; sportsbooks and Polymarket both cluster around 19%, roughly 11 percentage points higher. Both markets agree on Netherlands at 56–57%, well below the model's 68%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which reading is more accurate—the disagreement suggests material uncertainty about Sweden's true strength relative to the statistical estimate.
